notes: Smallest tool-less round-trip pinning the adapter-specific terminal end signal. For opencode that signal is the step-finish part with reason="stop": the parser maps it to a turn_done event (parser.go parseStepFinish: case "stop" → EventType "turn_done"), which settles the turn working→ready. Session state lives in opencode's SQLite store (~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db), observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as a UUID-keyed ses_… session. Overlaps intentionally with basic-turn; this cell focuses on the end-signal mechanism rather than the broader lifecycle. Phases assert the ready→working→ready arc: (1) session born in ready, (2) PID bound, (3) prompt drives ready→working, (4) the terminal-text turn completes back to ready when step-finish reason=stop closes the turn. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession (if the process scanner emits one) cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity that turn1_done chains off. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant covers tool_calls_max:0 — a no-tool, no-trailing-question turn never gates on a permission ask or read a waiting cue.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
User prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
The terminal-text turn completes back to ready on the same ses_… session when step-finish reason=stop closes the turn
